questions.question: "Marcha Filipina Magdalo" was the original title of the Philippines' national anthem before it was changed to "Marcha Nacional Filipina" and then to "Lupang Hinirang."

answer_key.explanation: Julian Felipe, a composer from Cavite, wrote the national anthem. He submitted the first draft to Emilio Aguinaldo.
There are 17 regions and within the regions are 81 provinces. The provinces are divided into 115 cities and 1,495 municipalities.

questions.question: Who was the "Hero of Tirad Pass"?

answer_key.explanation: He was born in San Jose, Bulacan on November 14, 1875. He died on December 2, 1899.

questions.question: The Mayon Volcano is located in what province in Region V?

answer_key.explanation: Mayon Volcano is known for its almost perfect shape. Its height is 2,460 meters.

questions.question: Which Philippine hero was born in Ilocos Norte and was also known for his "Spoliarium"?

answer_key.explanation: Juan Luna's painting "Spoliarium" won a gold medal in the Exposicion Nacional de Bellas Artes in Madrid in 1884.

questions.question: What is the "world's smallest commercial fish" that can be found in Bicol called?

answer_key.explanation: Lake Buhi of Camarines Sur is known as the habitat of sinarapan.

questions.question: In which town is the "First Monument of Rizal" located?

answer_key.explanation: This was built in 1898 across the Old Daet Municipal Hall. Daet is the capital of the province of Camarines Norte in the Bicol region.

questions.question: How many cities and municipalities does the National Capital Region (NCR) have?

answer_key.explanation: Manila, one of the cities in NCR, is the country's capital. There are 16 cities and 1 municipality.

questions.question: What are the official languages of the Philippines?

answer_key.explanation: Filipino is based on Tagalog. Former President Manuel L. Quezon was known as "Ama Ng Wikang Pambansa" because during his presidency they declared Filipino as the national language.

questions.question: Who is considered the second president of the Philippines?

answer_key.explanation: Manuel Quezon was the first president of the Commonwealth of the Philippines. He is considered the second President of the Philippines, after Emilio Aguinaldo.
